title: Everything is an Object 🧩
description: A quick look at Ruby's core philosophy: Everything is an object.

slides.title: The Golden Rule of Ruby 🥇
slides.content:
# Objects Everywhere!

In some programming languages, primitive things like numbers and words aren't very smart.

But in Ruby, the absolute golden rule is this:

**Everything is an object.**

Even a simple number like `5` is an object! Which means `5` has special powers and built-in methods it can perform.

slides.title: Numbers Have Superpowers
slides.content:
Look at what happens when we ask a number to run its built-in commands (methods).

Notice the `.` (dot) notation. This tells an object to perform a method.

slides.title: Quick Check 🧠
slides.content: Let's check your understanding of objects.

quizData.question: What is the core philosophy of Ruby?

options.text: Numbers and strings are primitives, everything else is an object.
Everything is an object, including numbers.

quizData.explanation: In Ruby, literally everything you interact with, including primitive types from other languages, is treated as a fully featured object with its own methods.

slides.title: Dot Notation 🎯
slides.content:
You invoke (call) an object's method by using a dot `.` followed by the method name.

`object.method_name`

For example, Strings (text) have lots of methods.

* `"hello".upcase` converts it to shouting.
* `"hello".length` tells us how many letters it has.

slides.title: String Methods
slides.content: Run this to see string methods in action!
slides.codeSnippet:
puts "ruby is awesome".upcase
puts "reverse this".reverse
puts "count me".length

slides.title: Quick Check 🧠
slides.content: How do you call a method?

quizData.question: Which punctuation mark is used to call a method on an object?
quizData.explanation: You use dot notation `.` to give objects an instruction (call one of its methods).

slides.title: Don't sweat the details! 😅
slides.content: We'll be exploring Objects and Methods in massive detail later. For now, just remember that pieces of data in Ruby are extremely capable and smart.
